commit.message:
Session 166: Smart gap classification — timing vs actionable vs irreducible

Add S3c to postPipelineAudit360.cjs that classifies orphan teams into:
- Timing: league has no recent matches (>45 days) — season transition, self-resolving
- Actionable: league HAS recent matches but orphan can't link — team resolution failure
- Irreducible: league has zero matches on platform — nothing to scrape

Update generateDashboard.cjs to display the classification breakdown:
- North Star now shows actionable/timing/irreducible counts below the total gap %
- Gap Anatomy table adds Actionable Gap, Timing Gap, Irreducible Gap rows
- Recommendation triggers on >2000 actionable orphans specifically

Current classification: 6,603 actionable (56%) | 3,950 timing (34%) | 1,201 irreducible (10%)

commit.message:
Mix editorial sections with relevant article cards inline

Each ## thematic section now renders as prose (left) + sidebar of the
articles cited in that section (right), giving a true newspaper layout
where related articles appear alongside the text that discusses them.
Uncited articles fall into a More Stories grid at the bottom.

- _parseSections(): splits LLM markdown by ## headings, extracts cited
  article indices per section
- _renderSectionProse(): renders prose with inline citation links
- _renderInlineCard(): compact sidebar article card
- _buildNpBody(): assembles section blocks, tracks rendered ids, appends
  uncited remainder as story grid
- Replaced above-fold/editorial-col/lead-story CSS with np-section-block /
  np-section-row / np-section-cards / np-inline-card layout
- Responsive: stacked on mobile, cards go horizontal row
- Print: section cards fixed width, prose 2-col on full-width sections

feat: improve package metadata, versioning, and API completeness (#26)

## Summary

Addresses findings from an OSS best-practices audit. No breaking
changes.

## Changes

### `pyproject.toml` — discoverability and dependency hygiene
- Added `keywords` for PyPI search (`apicurio`, `avro`,
`schema-registry`, `kafka`, `serialization`, `confluent`)
- Added full `classifiers` (Development Status, Audience, License,
Python versions 3.10–3.13, Topic, Typing)
- Added upper bounds on core dependencies: `fastavro>=1.9.0,<2.0` and
`httpx>=0.27.0,<1.0` — prevents silent breakage from major upstream
bumps
- Added `Changelog` entry to `[project.urls]`

### `src/apicurio_serdes/_version.py` + `__init__.py`
- New `_version.py` with `__version__ = "0.1.0"` as the single source of
truth
- `__version__` exported from `__init__.py` — callers can now do
`apicurio_serdes.__version__`

### `CHANGELOG.md`
- Added root-level `CHANGELOG.md` (Keep a Changelog format) — mirrors
`docs/changelog.en.md`
- This is the standard location tools and users expect;
`docs/changelog.en.md` remains for the bilingual docs site

### `src/apicurio_serdes/_client.py`
- Added `close()`, `__enter__`, and `__exit__` to
`ApicurioRegistryClient`
- Restores symmetry with `AsyncApicurioRegistryClient` which already has
`aclose` / `__aenter__` / `__aexit__`
- Enables `with ApicurioRegistryClient(...) as client:` usage for
explicit resource cleanup

### `README.md`
- Updated feature status table — deserializer and async client were
listed as "in progress" but both shipped in 0.1.0

### Tests
- Added `test_context_manager_closes_client` to `tests/test_client.py`
- All 235 tests pass at 100% branch coverage

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes
- [ ] Verify `pip show apicurio-serdes` shows classifiers after next
PyPI release
- [ ] Confirm `python -c "import apicurio_serdes;
print(apicurio_serdes.__version__)"` works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Construct URLs from db_map — eliminate per-case metadata lookups

The per-case caseBrowse metadata calls were returning 404 for most cases.
New approach: fetch the full database list once to build a db_id → jurisdiction
map, then construct URLs directly from db_id + case_id + year. This removes
hundreds of API calls and fixes the 404 issue entirely.

commit.message:
feat(community): add and polish community health documents (#25)

## Summary

Brings the [GitHub community health
score](https://github.com/jcbianic/apicurio-serdes/community) from 57%
to 100% by adding all missing standard files, then refining them based
on a critical review against best practices in the Python OSS ecosystem
(httpx, pydantic, ruff).

## Files added / changed

| File | Status | What changed |
|---|---|---|
| `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` | New → revised | Contributor Covenant 2.1.
Enforcement routes to **maintainer email**, not Security Advisories
(which are for vulnerabilities, not CoC violations) |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | New → revised | Adds uv primer, project philosophy
(100% coverage rationale, sync/async parity), explicit scope boundaries
("what NOT to contribute"), response time SLAs |
| `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md` | New → revised | Added: client
type (sync/async), caching context (fresh vs cached), explicit traceback
field, guidance on what a "minimal reproducer" means |
| `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md` | New → revised | Added:
backward-compatibility question, workaround context, version field |
| `.github/pull_request_template.md` | New → revised | Added:
type-of-change checklist, lint + typecheck items, breaking-change flag,
Conventional Commits reminder |
| `README.md` | Modified | Replaced maintainer-only release instructions
with a Contributing link |
| `DEVELOPMENT.md` | New | Maintainer-facing release guide (version bump
→ CHANGELOG → tag → publish) |

## What was wrong with v1

The initial draft had two significant errors caught by a critical
review:

1. **CODE_OF_CONDUCT** routed Code of Conduct violations to GitHub
Security Advisories — a category error. Security Advisories are for
embargoed vulnerability reports, not community governance.
2. **pull_request_template** omitted lint and typecheck from the
checklist, giving contributors an incomplete picture of CI gates.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes
- [ ] Visit [community health
profile](https://github.com/jcbianic/apicurio-serdes/community) after
merge and confirm 100%
- [ ] Open a test issue to verify templates render correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

docs: add planning & pre-coding phase improvements proposal (10 items)

Identifies gaps in cloudy's planning/design phase vs superpowers patterns:
- Codebase exploration before planning
- Baseline test run as pre-execution gate
- Human approval checkpoint
- TDD as numbered task sub-steps
- Design rationale document
- Sequential clarifying questions
- Git worktree isolation per task
- Task granularity guidance
- Testing anti-patterns in executor context
- Pre-planning brainstorming gate

commit.message:
fix: use safe slugs for product categories to prevent 404 errors

Category slugs from Wix contained URL-encoded characters (e.g. %26 for &)
which caused 404s on static export. Now uses safeSlug() to normalize them
(e.g. sealants-and-tapes instead of sealants-%26-tapes).

commit.message:
fix: standardize ECI handling to use ncf (non-point CFs) consistently

Both CVM and MCS now validate and use ncf ECIs instead of tc:
- CVM already used ncf from Stage 2 for validation
- MCS now also loads AllClusterData and validates eci.length == ncf
- CEC JSON files now store only ncf values (4 instead of 6)
- Removed point and empty cluster ECIs (constants, not optimization variables)

This eliminates the design inconsistency where CVM used 4 ECIs but
MCS accepted 6. Both now expect ncf-length ECI arrays.

Files changed:
- MCSCalculationContext: load AllClusterData, override getClusterTypeCount() to return ncf
- CalculationService: set AllClusterData on MCS context, use ncf for requiredECILength
- cec.json: store only 4 values (ncf) instead of 6 (tc)

- session/page.js: "Save & Exit" button in header, collapsible text input for beginners
- review/page.js: PrintableWorksheet + BookRecommendation components integrated
- profile/page.js: 4-column badge grid with ACHIEVEMENT_CATALOGUE mapping,
  locked badges shown with opacity-40+grayscale

Phase 4 — Admin & Parent Dashboard:
- NEW frontend/src/app/parent/page.js: parent dashboard (child selector tabs,
  stats grid, recent sessions, AI feedback card, notifications with unread badge)
- NEW frontend/src/app/admin/students/page.js: full CRUD with live API
  (list, search, add, edit, delete), LoadingCard skeleton, auth redirect
- backend/src/routes/admin.js: requireAdmin RBAC applied in production,
  GET/POST/PUT /students endpoints
- backend/src/middleware/auth.js: requireAdmin + requireParent middleware exports
- frontend/src/app/layout.js: Parent nav link (shows only for parent/admin role),
  reads userRole from sessionStorage

Phase 5 — Security, COPPA, CI/CD:
- NEW frontend/src/app/consent/page.js: COPPA parental consent form (3 checkboxes
  + legal name + electronic signature submit → POST /api/auth/consent)
- NEW frontend/src/app/privacy-policy/page.js: full privacy policy with COPPA
  statement, data use, parental rights, contact info
- NEW supabase/migrations/004_coppa_consent.sql: coppa_consent columns on parents,
  consent_audit_log (append-only RLS), students/sessions RLS policies,
  DB CHECK constraints (content≤2000, word≤100 chars)
- NEW .github/workflows/ci.yml: 4-job CI pipeline (backend tests, frontend build,
  npm audit security scan, Docker build on main)
- backend/src/routes/auth.js: POST /consent endpoint (parent update + audit log)
- backend/src/middleware/sanitize.js: inputLengthLimiter (10KB body, 1000 char fields),
  profanityFilter (asterisk replacement, non-blocking)
- backend/src/lib/config.js: validateEnv() with per-environment required var list
- backend/src/app.js: validateEnv() at startup, full middleware chain order

critic: update compact context with FULL_8000 and oscillation analysis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ml): multi-worker model reload via sentinel file + prediction quality fixes

Sentinel file mechanism:
- After training completes, write active version to MODEL_DIR/active_version.txt
- Every predict request calls _maybe_reload_model() which reads the sentinel
- All uvicorn workers converge to the new model on their next request
- Replaces broken `global model = new_model` pattern that only updated
  the single worker running the training thread

Also fixes caught in code review:
- fix: DOW convention mismatch in volatility_weekday/weekend split
  (Postgres EXTRACT(DOW) = 0=Sun,6=Sat, not pandas 5=Sat,6=Sun)
- fix: .squeeze(axis=2) crash guard for CatBoost VirtEnsembles 2D output
- fix: avg_wait_same_dow_4w computed per prediction timestamp (not broadcast
  from base_time_local across all 24 future hours — train/inference skew)
- perf: replace O(n_rows) DataFrame filter loop with O(1) dict lookup
  for avg_wait_same_dow_4w (was causing CPU saturation + service hang)

New training features (effective from v20260309_0600):
- volatility_weekday / volatility_weekend split
- rolling_avg_weekday / rolling_avg_weekend (SQL window functions)
- avg_wait_same_dow_4w (mean of last 4 same-DOW observations)
- VOLATILITY_CAP_STD_MINUTES lowered 40 → 15

commit.message:
Release v4.6.0: Conflict Resolution UX (Goal 28)

Stop silent confidence decay and surface conflict resolution to users.

## Added

- **Conflict persistence**: detectConflicts() results now accumulated to project
  metadata via persistNewConflicts() with deduplication. Removes silent decay
  of older memories when contradictions detected.

- **recall output_mode=conflicts**: New mode to inspect all unresolved
  contradictions between memories. Shows similarity, reason, NLI scores, and
  LLM explanations (if available).

- **memory action=resolve_conflict**: New action to resolve detected contradictions.
  Four strategies:
  - keep_newer: deprecate the older memory
  - keep_older: deprecate the newer memory
  - keep_both: acknowledge conflict, retain both
  - merge: consolidate via existing consolidate action

- **Briefing surface**: Full-tier session briefings now include
  unresolvedConflicts count when contradictions exist.

## Changed

- Removed applyConflictConfidenceDecay() calls from conflict detection path
  (function definition kept for future use). Users now have explicit control
  via resolve_conflict instead of automatic 20% decay on each recall.

## Tests

Added 16 tests covering:
- persistNewConflicts deduplication and accumulation
- recall conflicts output mode validation
- resolve_conflict validation (params, conflicts, strategies)
- detectConflicts no longer produces dirtyItems

Full test suite: 118 files, 2206 tests passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CSS cascade layers and tools filter dark mode

Wrap global base styles (body, headings) in @layer base and
component styles (layout, prose, transitions) in @layer components
so Tailwind utilities can properly override them via the cascade.
Previously, unlayered CSS always beat layered Tailwind utilities
regardless of specificity, causing dark mode text classes to fail.

Also fix the tools category filter JS to set complete className
strings with dark: variants instead of toggling individual classes
that lacked dark mode support.

Users can now set custom hotkeys (including modifier combos like
Cmd+Shift+R) for toggle and stop actions via the menu bar interface.
Settings persist in ~/Library/Application Support/Whispr/config.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add LokiLogger - Erlang logger handler that pushes logs to Loki

Registers as an Erlang :logger handler, buffers log messages, and
flushes to Loki in batches via HTTP. No Promtail needed. Configurable
min_level, flush_interval, and max_buffer_size. Starts automatically
when loki_url is configured via the supervisor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ios): crash on launch — GoogleService-Info.plist missing from Xcode project (#110)

* fix(ios): add GoogleService-Info.plist to Xcode project to fix launch crash

GoogleService-Info.plist existed on disk but was never registered in
project.pbxproj, so it was not copied into the app bundle.
FirebaseApp.configure() in AppDelegate could not find it and called
fatalError(), crashing the app immediately on launch.

Changes:
- Register GoogleService-Info.plist in PBXFileReference, PBXBuildFile,
  PBXGroup, and PBXResourcesBuildPhase so it is bundled with the app
- Register App.entitlements in the project and add CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS
  build setting (Debug + Release) so Associated Domains work
- Add safety check in setup-native.sh that warns if GoogleService-Info.plist
  is missing from the Xcode project
- Bump version to 1.0.7 (build 10007)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ios): fail build if GoogleService-Info.plist is missing from Xcode project

Better to catch this at build time than discover it as a crash in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show cancelled badge for members with pending cancellation and filter from bulk messages

- Display "Cancelled" badge on member detail page when subscription_cancel_at is set
- Filter cancelled members from "Select All Active" in bulk message modal
- Fetch subscription_cancel_at from accounts table for both pages
- Update member count in bulk message modal to show active members only

This ensures cancelled members (even with pending end dates) are clearly identified
and excluded from bulk communications.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Create landing page with AI agents statement and hero section
- Create login/register page with clean form UI
- Redesign entire UI: cream backgrounds, green accents, serif display
  headings, rounded cards, soft shadows (inspired by Akademia)
- Update Navbar with user name display and logout
- Update tailwind config with new color palette and typography
